grades.overview: Build deep number sense to 20 with concrete models, oral language, and picture reasoning.
bigIdeas: Addition and subtraction are actions in stories
Shapes can be sorted by attributes

lessons.title: Count and Match to 10
lessons.objective: Count objects with one-to-one correspondence and match numerals to quantities.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use counters, then dot cards, then numerals to represent the same quantity.

lessons.title: Compose and Decompose 10
lessons.objective: Represent number pairs that make 10 using objects and drawings.
lessons.activityPrompt: Show 10 as two parts in multiple ways and explain each with sentence frames.

lessons.title: Story Problems Within 10
lessons.objective: Solve add-to and take-from stories with drawings and manipulatives.
lessons.activityPrompt: Act out a short story, draw it, then write the equation that matches.

lessons.title: Compare Groups
lessons.objective: Use language more than, less than, and equal to compare sets.
lessons.activityPrompt: Build two sets and justify which has more using matching strategy.

lessons.title: Classify Shapes by Attributes
lessons.objective: Sort 2D and 3D shapes by side count, corners, and faces.
lessons.activityPrompt: Sort mixed shape cards and explain rules in a class math talk.

lessons.title: Teen Number Intro
lessons.objective: Build teen numbers as ten ones and some extra ones.
lessons.activityPrompt: Bundle 10 straws and add singles to represent each teen number.

lessons.title: Addition Within 5
lessons.objective: Add two groups with a total of 5 or less using objects and drawings.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use counters to act out addition stories and write the equation.

lessons.title: Addition Within 10
lessons.objective: Add two groups with a total of 10 or less using a ten frame.
lessons.activityPrompt: Fill a ten frame and write the matching addition equation.

grades.overview: Connect counting fluency to place value and story-based operations within 20 and 100.
bigIdeas: Operations can be modeled multiple ways
Explanations matter as much as answers

lessons.title: Subtraction Stories Within 20
lessons.objective: Use drawings and equations to solve subtraction stories within 20.
lessons.activityPrompt: Represent each story using counters, a bar model, and an equation.

lessons.title: Fact Families and Number Bonds
lessons.objective: Relate addition and subtraction using part-part-whole structure.
lessons.activityPrompt: Complete missing-number triangles and create equation families.

lessons.title: Teen Place Value Core
lessons.objective: Represent 11-19 as one ten and some ones.
lessons.activityPrompt: Build teen numbers with rods and cubes, then sketch quick pictures.

lessons.title: Compare Two-Digit Numbers
lessons.objective: Compare two-digit numbers by tens first and ones next.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use place-value mats to justify each comparison statement.

lessons.title: Add and Subtract Within 100
lessons.objective: Use place-value strategies for adding and subtracting within 100.
lessons.activityPrompt: Solve by making a ten and explain strategy choices to a partner.

lessons.title: Measure Length with Iteration
lessons.objective: Measure objects with repeated units and compare lengths.
lessons.activityPrompt: Measure classroom objects with paper clips and explain why gaps matter.

lessons.title: Addition Within 20
lessons.objective: Add fluently within 20 using strategies like make-ten.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use a number line to add and record equations.

lessons.title: Fact Families
lessons.objective: Find the missing addend using part-part-whole relationships.
lessons.activityPrompt: Complete number bond triangles and write all four related equations.

grades.overview: Develop fluency and reasoning with base-ten structure, regrouping, and equal groups.
bigIdeas: Base-ten representations support mental and written methods
Regrouping reflects quantity, not a trick
Models support transfer to abstract notation

lessons.title: Place Value to 1,000
lessons.objective: Read, write, and represent numbers to 1,000.
lessons.activityPrompt: Compose and decompose numbers with hundreds flats, tens rods, and ones.

lessons.title: Compare Three-Digit Numbers
lessons.objective: Compare numbers using place-value reasoning and symbols.
lessons.activityPrompt: Explain comparison decisions with place-value language and diagrams.

lessons.title: Add/Subtract with Regrouping
lessons.objective: Add and subtract within 1,000 using concrete and written methods.
lessons.activityPrompt: Trade tens and ones with blocks before writing vertical equations.

lessons.title: Equal Groups Foundations
lessons.objective: Use repeated addition and arrays to model equal groups.
lessons.activityPrompt: Build arrays with tiles and write matching repeated-addition equations.

lessons.title: Time and Money Reasoning
lessons.objective: Solve time intervals and money combinations with visual models.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use open number lines for elapsed time and coin-value decompositions.

lessons.title: Represent Data
lessons.objective: Create simple line plots and answer comparison questions.
lessons.activityPrompt: Collect class data and justify statements using evidence from plots.

lessons.title: Add Within 100
lessons.objective: Add two-digit numbers using place value strategies.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use base-ten blocks to add, then record the algorithm.

lessons.title: Subtract Within 100
lessons.objective: Subtract two-digit numbers with and without regrouping.
lessons.activityPrompt: Model regrouping with blocks before moving to the written algorithm.

grades.overview: Transition to multiplication/division, fractions, and model-based multi-step problem solving.
bigIdeas: Equal groups and arrays unlock multiplication and division
Fractions are numbers that can be represented and compared

lessons.title: Meaning of Multiplication
lessons.objective: Interpret products as equal groups and arrays.
lessons.activityPrompt: Move from counters to array drawings to symbolic multiplication equations.

lessons.title: Meaning of Division
lessons.objective: Interpret quotient as sharing and grouping.
lessons.activityPrompt: Model both partitive and quotative division with bar models.

lessons.title: Fractions as Unit Fractions
lessons.objective: Understand fractions as equal parts and unit fractions on number lines.
lessons.activityPrompt: Partition strips, then locate fractions on open number lines.

lessons.title: Compare Fractions
lessons.objective: Compare fractions with same numerator or denominator.
lessons.activityPrompt: Analyze wrong comparison statements and fix reasoning with visuals.

lessons.title: Area as Tiling
lessons.objective: Measure area by counting square units and composing rectangles.
lessons.activityPrompt: Tile rectangles physically, then derive area multiplication patterns.

lessons.title: Two-Step Story Problems
lessons.objective: Represent and solve two-step problems with equations and models.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use bar models to map known/unknown quantities before calculating.

grades.overview: Strengthen place-value fluency with larger numbers, fraction operations foundations, and precise reasoning.
bigIdeas: Place value extends to large numbers and decimals

lessons.title: Place Value to 1,000,000
lessons.objective: Read, write, compare, and round multi-digit whole numbers.
lessons.activityPrompt: Generate near-miss numbers to discuss rounding boundary decisions.

lessons.title: Multi-Digit Multiplication
lessons.objective: Multiply up to 4-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit numbers.
lessons.activityPrompt: Transition from area models to algorithm while preserving place-value meaning.

lessons.title: Division with Partial Quotients
lessons.objective: Divide multi-digit numbers by one-digit divisors with reasoning.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use open arrays and partial quotients before compact notation.

lessons.title: Fraction Equivalence and Ordering
lessons.objective: Generate equivalent fractions and compare them on number lines.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use strip models to justify why two fractions are equivalent.

lessons.title: Add/Subtract Fractions
lessons.objective: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators.
lessons.activityPrompt: Start with visual models and identify common denominator mistakes.

lessons.title: Angle Measurement and Geometry
lessons.objective: Measure and draw angles; solve unknown-angle problems.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use protractors and angle decomposition challenges to reason.

grades.overview: Prepare for middle-school readiness with fractions, decimals, volume, and proportional reasoning foundations.
bigIdeas: Fractions and decimals are connected representations
Operations are justified with models and properties
Students critique and revise mathematical arguments

lessons.title: Place Value with Decimals
lessons.objective: Understand decimal place value to thousandths and compare decimals.
lessons.activityPrompt: Compare close decimals and justify using place-value language.

lessons.title: Add/Subtract Fractions with Unlike Denominators
lessons.objective: Find equivalent fractions and compute sums/differences.
lessons.activityPrompt: Model denominator alignment visually before symbolic work.

lessons.title: Multiply Fractions and Whole Numbers
lessons.objective: Interpret and compute fraction multiplication using area and scaling.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use area models and number lines to explain each product.

lessons.title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
lessons.objective: Find volume by counting unit cubes and using formulas.
lessons.activityPrompt: Build layer diagrams and connect to l x w x h formula.

lessons.title: Line Plots with Fraction Data
lessons.objective: Interpret fractional data on line plots and solve problems.
lessons.activityPrompt: Use line-plot evidence to justify operations on fractional data.

lessons.title: Ratios and Patterns Bridge
lessons.objective: Analyze numerical patterns and prepare for ratio reasoning.
lessons.activityPrompt: Generate and explain pattern rules with tables and expressions.
